---
# Multi-branch classification with nested if() (case is NOT supported)

XQL has `if(<cond>, <then>, <else>)` but NO `case()` — `case(...)` returns a generic 500. Build multi-way classification by NESTING `if` in the else slot: `if(c1, "H", if(c2, "M", "L"))`. Verified: bytes>100000→H, >20000→M, else L mapped a 10-row set correctly. Readable for 2-4 tiers; for more branches consider a lookup-table join. Also live: `coalesce(<col>, <default>)` fills nulls with a fallback.

```sql
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.NETWORK
| comp sum(action_total_upload) as up by actor_process_image_name
| alter exfil_tier = if(up > 100000000, "CRITICAL", if(up > 10000000, "HIGH", if(up > 1000000, "MEDIUM", "low")))
| sort desc up
```

_Stage/function syntax live-verified on a real XSIAM tenant (Advanced-auth PAPI, narrow window). The mechanics are confirmed; adapt field names to your dataset._
